name: Herrera del Duque Castle and Sierra de la Ubría Loop
name_original: Sierra de la Ubría por el Castillo de Herrera del Duque — circular
description: Challenging 14.9-mile circular hike to Castillo de Herrera del Duque, offering panoramic views and diverse landscapes in Sierra de la Ubría.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Herrera Del Duque traverse a diverse landscape within the La Siberia Extremeña Biosphere Reserve in Extremadura, Spain. The region features Mediterranean forests, mountain ranges like the Sierra de la Rinconada, and significant water bodies including the Guadiana River and García de Sola Reservoir. Hikers encounter varied terrain, from riverine paths to elevated viewpoints offering panoramic vistas. The area is characterized by a mix of pastureland, mountains, and dense woodlands, supporting a rich array of wildlife. Best hiking trails in Herrera Del Duque The most popular hiking route is Castillo de Herrera del Duque from Herrera del Duque – round trip via Sierra del Chamorro, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the historic castle and surrounding hills. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mirador de la Rinconada Trail, a moderate 10.0 miles (16.0 km) path. This trail leads through the Mediterranean forests of the Sierra de la Rinconada, providing elevated perspectives of the landscape. Local hikers also love the Camino al Castillejo Trail, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain with reservoir views, often completed in about 2 hours 16 minutes. Hiking around Herrera Del Duque is defined by Mediterranean forests, river valleys, and mountain ranges, offering routes for various ability levels. The network includes easy walks and more challenging ascents. The routes in Herrera Del Duque are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 10 hikers have used komoot to explore Herrera Del Duque's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Herrera del Duque?
A: There are over 10 hiking trails around Herrera del Duque, offering a range of experiences from easy walks to more challenging ascents within the diverse landscapes of the La Siberia Extremeña Biosphere Reserve.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Herrera del Duque?
A: The terrain around Herrera del Duque is highly varied, encompassing Mediterranean forests, mountain ranges like the Sierra de la Rinconada and Sierra de las Barbas de Oro, and significant water bodies such as the Guadiana River and García de Sola Reservoir. You'll find paths through pastureland, dense woodlands, and along river valleys, often leading to elevated viewpoints.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Herrera del Duque?
A: Yes, there are several easy trails. For instance, the Herrera del Duque Town Center Loop is an easy 5.11 km hike. The region offers 2 easy routes, perfect for a relaxed walk.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: Hikers can explore the Sierra de la Rinconada and Sierra de las Barbas de Oro mountain ranges, offering panoramic views. The Guadiana River and García de Sola Reservoir are prominent natural attractions, surrounded by diverse forests. You can also find historical sites like the 15th-16th-century fortress-castle on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101999807/castillo-de-herrera-del-duque-from-herrera-del-duque-round-trip-via-sierra-del-chamorro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Herrera del Duque from Herrera del Duque – round trip via Sierra del Chamorro&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many trails are designed as circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101999807/castillo-de-herrera-del-duque-from-herrera-del-duque-round-trip-via-sierra-del-chamorro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Herrera del Duque from Herrera del Duque – round trip via Sierra del Chamorro&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the historic castle and surrounding hills.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Herrera del Duque?
A: Spring and autumn are generally recommended for hiking in Herrera del Duque. The weather is milder, and the natural landscapes are particularly vibrant. The rutting season for large game animals in the Cíjara Regional Hunting Reserve, particularly in September and October, offers unique wildlife observation opportunities.

Q: Can I find trails with good viewpoints in Herrera del Duque?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its stunning vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1268822548/mirador-de-la-rinconada-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Rinconada Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads through Mediterranean forests and provides elevated perspectives. Other notable lookouts include Consolación Pass Lookout and Navas Pass, offering views of the Guadiana River and surrounding villages.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options?
A: While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed, the area offers 2 easy routes that could be suitable for families. The diverse landscapes and opportunities for wildlife spotting along rivers and reservoirs can make for an engaging experience for children.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Herrera del Duque is rich in wildlife. You might spot griffon vultures, black vultures, black storks, golden eagles, and peregrine falcons, especially around the García de Sola Reservoir. Large game animals like deer and wild boars can be observed in areas like the Cíjara Regional Hunting Reserve, particularly during the rutting season.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water bodies or offer opportunities for wild swimming?
A: Many routes follow the Guadiana River and its reservoirs, such as the García de Sola Reservoir and Cíjara Reservoir. While specific wild swimming spots are not detailed, these water bodies provide beautiful scenery and opportunities for enjoying the natural environment. The eco-path of the Guadalupejo River is also an official bird sanctuary.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Herrera del Duque?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 3 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from river valleys to mountain ranges, and the rich natural beauty of the La Siberia Extremeña Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, there is 1 difficult route available. The region's mountainous areas and longer trails, such as the 14.9-mile (24 km) Castillo de Herrera del Duque Circular Hike mentioned in regional research, offer more strenuous options with significant elevation changes.
tips:
The Herrera del Duque Castle, located in the province of Badajoz (Extremadura), is an impressive 15th-century fortress that dominates the landscape of the La Siberia region from the top of the Sierra Chamorro.
It is said that wailing sounds can be heard at night from the "Tower of Sighs," and there are legends about a hidden treasure protected by a curse.

Close to Fuenlabrada de los Montes, in the southwest, we find a mountain range that is divided into a pair, the southernmost one, called the Sierra de la Ubría. To the north is the Sierra del Chamorro.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de la Ubría por el Castillo de Herrera del Duque — circular trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered difficult hiking due to its 24 km length and approximately 540 meters of elevation gain. It requires good physical condition. While there are shorter, easier rural walks around Herrera del Duque, this specific circular route is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies significantly, transitioning from wide pastures and dehesas to dense pine forests and Mediterranean scrub. You'll encounter uneven paths, especially on the short but steep ascent to the Castillo de Herrera del Duque, which can be slippery. Appropriate footwear is essential.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The most notable feature is the impressive medieval Castillo de Herrera del Duque, offering grandiose panoramic views of the town and the vast La Siberia region. You'll also traverse the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de la Ubría, part of the Cíjara Regional Reserve, and pass through sections near the Herrera del Duque Town Center.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The starting point for this circular route is often near a parking area in or around Herrera del Duque. Specific parking details may vary, but generally, you can find convenient spots close to the town center or the trail's initial access points.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers different experiences throughout the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for hiking. Summers can be very hot in Extremadura, making early morning starts or avoiding the peak summer months advisable. Winters are typically mild but can be wet.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de la Ubría por el Castillo de Herrera del Duque — circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, specific regulations regarding dogs are not detailed. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to ensure you clean up after them. Always check local signage for any restrictions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this route?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this particular trail or access the Castillo de Herrera del Duque or the Sierra de la Ubría. However, it's always good practice to respect the natural environment and any local guidelines.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plants might I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de la Ubría is rich in biodiversity. You might spot vegetation like thyme, broom, and elm trees. Wildlife can include black-headed warblers, necked terrapins, and bee-eaters. The region is also known for birds of prey, woodpeckers, deer, wild boars, and foxes.

faqs.question: Are there any alternative, shorter hikes in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, if the full 24 km circular route is too challenging, there are shorter, easier rural walks around Herrera del Duque that still incorporate the castle. One such route is approximately 8 kilometers long, takes about 3 hours, and is rated as 'easy' with moderate effort.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, wear sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support. Layers of clothing are recommended to adapt to changing weather. Bring plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a map or GPS device. Trekking poles can be helpful for the steep sections.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route also passes through sections of the Camino del Levante - Etapa 3, covering approximately 6% of its length.
